title: SIM: Sales Incentive Machine — OpenAI Build Week Demo
description: A reproducible 2:30 product demo assembled from runbook evidence and focused live captures.

scenes.title: From whiteboard guesswork to one workspace
scenes.narration:
Restaurant sales contests still live on whiteboards, manual tallies, and whoever remembers what changed.
SIM replaces the guesswork with one auditable workspace.

capture.instructions: Copy without altering product content; frame the active Corn Cup Countdown card and the top of the leaderboard.

scenes.title: Three-command local start
scenes.narration:
SIM starts with three commands: install, seed, and run.
Sample data makes every screen useful instantly, and managers import their own sales through the shared spreadsheet schema.

capture.instructions: Record a clean terminal running npm install, npm run seed, and npm run dev, then hard-cut within the clip to the first successful dashboard load. Hide usernames, filesystem paths, environment values, and all secrets.

scenes.title: Live performance at a glance
scenes.narration:
One dashboard ranks every server across live sales metrics, contest goals, and daily Bingo wins.
Managers can switch the performance lens while the active prize, targets, last winner, and deadline stay visible.
Every downstream game reads the same sales records.

capture.instructions: Begin on the Corn Cup Countdown hero, then change the performance metric once and make one slow vertical move through the leaderboard. Use the runbook still as the framing reference or silent fallback.

scenes.title: GPT-5.6 contest design, validated locally
scenes.narration:
With an API key, GPT-5.6 receives the manager goal, menu, and four-week performance context.
Strict JSON schema constrains the response, then local validation checks every menu ID and contest rule.
Validation failure retries once. Without a key, or after two failed attempts, SIM loads its versioned sample config.
The API-backed draft is reviewed and activated through manual Contest setup.

capture.instructions: Truthfully demonstrate the API-backed capability: show a sanitized terminal request containing only a fake manager goal and the GPT-5.6 strict-schema response label, then cut to the populated manual Contest setup UI. Do not imply the current UI contains a prompt field. Show the keyless sample label in a separate sanitized request or caption; never reveal a key.

scenes.title: Live races and shared goals
scenes.narration:
Sales games turn the same live totals into a visible floor race and a shared goal board.
Final awards become wheel entries, keeping every contest mechanic connected and auditable.

capture.instructions: Create a 16:9 crop from the top of the final Gameboards evidence, retaining both the Final race result and the beginning of the awarded goal board. Do not stretch the source.

scenes.title: Bingo from card to daily win
scenes.narration:
Every server gets a randomized five-by-five card drawn from the active contest's menu pool.
Managers print one clean card per page, then log completed lines and daily wins when cards return.

capture.instructions: Record one quick row-mark gesture, show the completed-line state, then open print preview long enough to prove the one-card-per-page layout. Use only fictional seeded names and menu items.

scenes.title: One auditable drawing
scenes.narration:
Before drawing, the contender panel explains goal, Bingo, and game entries for each server.
One confirmed draw saves an immutable snapshot first; only then does the wheel animate toward the recorded winner.
The presentation stays exciting without letting animation decide the result.

capture.instructions: Use a fresh disposable drawing state. Begin with one contender expanded, enter TV mode if framing improves, confirm exactly once, retain the full five-second spin, and hold on the persisted winner. Never reset or redraw the completed runbook database.

scenes.title: Fast entry with an audit trail
scenes.narration:
Shift leaders add contest-only quantities without inventing check data.
For complete records, SIM also imports CSVs and preserves corrections in an audit trail.

capture.instructions: Show the quantity-only contest tally and automatic total, then move to Import a sheet and the current-data correction controls. Do not submit another runbook operation and do not import real data.

scenes.title: What ships next
scenes.narration:
Next up: remote sales entry, a simple phone panel for shift managers that feeds the dashboard live.
Then reconciliation before finalizing, visible wheel odds, and role-guarded awards, all surfaced by real operator rehearsals.

card.title: What ships next
lines: Remote sales entry: a phone panel for shift managers, one simple HTML connection to the live dashboard.
Pre-finalization reconciliation and wheel provenance with visible odds.
Role-guarded lock and award controls, surfaced by live operator rehearsals.

capture.instructions: Generate a 1920-by-1080 SIM-branded roadmap card from the card fields.

scenes.title: Built in Codex with GPT-5.6
scenes.narration:
SIM turns restaurant sales into one connected, accountable weekly game.
Built in Codex with GPT-5.6; source and the reproducible demo pipeline are in the repository.

capture.instructions: Create a 1920-by-1080 SIM-branded end card reading Built in Codex with GPT-5.6. Add the verified public repository URL only after it is supplied; do not invent one.
